label: 3151a Veterinary medicine loan repayment

children.heading: Service in shortage situations
children.label: 1 Service in shortage situations
children.text: The Secretary shall carry out a program of entering into agreements with veterinarians under which the veterinarians agree to provide, for a period of time as determined by the Secretary and specified in the agreement, veterinary services in veterinarian shortage situations. For each year of such service under an agreement under this paragraph, the Secretary shall pay an amount, as determined by the Secretary and specified in the agreement, of the principal and interest of qualifying educational loans of the veterinarians.

children.heading: Service to Federal Government in emergency situations
children.label: 2 Service to Federal Government in emergency situations

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary may enter into agreements of 1 year duration with veterinarians who have agreements pursuant to paragraph (1) for such veterinarians to provide services to the Federal Government in emergency situations, as determined by the Secretary, under terms and conditions specified in the agreement. Pursuant to an agreement under this paragraph, the Secretary shall pay an amount, in addition to the amount paid pursuant to the agreement in paragraph (1), as determined by the Secretary and specified in the agreement, of the principal and interest of qualifying educational loans of the veterinarians.

children.label: i
children.text: A veterinarian shall not be required to serve more than 60 working days per year of the agreement.

children.label: ii
children.text: A veterinarian who provides service pursuant to the agreement shall receive a salary commensurate with the duties and shall be reimbursed for travel and per diem expenses as appropriate for the duration of the service.

children.heading: Determination of veterinarian shortage situations
children.label: b Determination of veterinarian shortage situations

children.text: geographical areas that the Secretary determines have a shortage of veterinarians; and
areas of veterinary practice that the Secretary determines have a shortage of veterinarians, such as food animal medicine, public health, epidemiology, and food safety.

children.heading: Authority
children.label: 1 Authority
children.text: The Secretary may carry out this program directly or enter into agreements with another Federal agency or other service provider to assist in the administration of this program.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Agreements with program participants shall provide remedies for any breach of an agreement by a participant, including repayment or partial repayment of financial assistance received, with interest.

children.heading: Amounts recovered
children.label: B Amounts recovered
children.text: Funds recovered under this subsection shall be credited to the account available to carry out this section and shall remain available until expended.

children.heading: Waiver
children.label: 3 Waiver
children.text: The Secretary may grant a waiver of the repayment obligation for breach of contract in the event of extreme hardship or extreme need, as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: affects the ability of the Secretary to maximize the number of agreements that can be provided under the Veterinary Medicine Loan Repayment Program from the amounts appropriated for such agreements; and

children.label: B
children.text: provides an incentive to serve in veterinary service shortage areas with the greatest need.

children.label: A
children.text: tuition expenses;

children.label: B
children.text: all other reasonable educational expenses, including fees, books, and laboratory expenses, incurred by the individual; or

children.label: C
children.text: reasonable living expenses as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Repayment schedule
children.label: 6 Repayment schedule
children.text: The Secretary may enter into an agreement with the holder of any loan for which payments are made under this section to establish a schedule for the making of such payments.

children.heading: Tax liability
children.label: 7 Tax liability
children.text: In addition to educational loan repayments, the Secretary shall make such additional payments to participants as the Secretary determines to be appropriate for the purpose of providing reimbursements to participants for individual tax liability resulting from participation in this program.

children.heading: Priority
children.label: 8 Priority
children.text: In administering the program, the Secretary shall give priority to agreements with veterinarians for the practice of food animal medicine in veterinarian shortage situations.

children.heading: Use of funds
children.label: d Use of funds
children.text: None of the funds appropriated to the Secretary under subsection (f) may be used to carry out .section 5379 of title 5

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: e Regulations
children.text: Notwithstanding subchapter II of chapter 5 of title 5, not later than 270 days after the date of enactment of this subsection, the Secretary shall promulgate regulations to carry out this section.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: f Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated for carrying out this section such sums as may be necessary and such sums shall remain available to the Secretary for the purposes of this section until expended.
